title: Significance of EVA 01 The Devil being Lilin rather than Adamite
display_title: Significance of EVA 01 The Devil being Lilin rather than Adamite
wikitext:
[]: Significance of EVA-01 ("The Devil") being Lilin rather than "Adamite"             Quote Post by Asunji_Yuko » Tue May 30, 2023 1:16 pm We know all EVAs are clones of Adam except for the main character's/Unit 01/Test Type/Shogohki/"The Devil," who is a clone of Lilith. What's the significance of this? I don't recall seeing it discussed in much detail or matter much in the original series Thoughts?

[]: Re: Significance of EVA-01 ("The Devil") being Lilin rather than "Adamite"             Quote Post by Blockio » Tue May 30, 2023 4:48 pm I'm not positive that this line in particular has a particularily strong tie to the fact that 01 is cloned out of Lilith, so much as it being contrasted with 01 being called God at several other points throughout; although I will agree that it is an interesting angle to think about

[]: Re: Significance of EVA-01 ("The Devil") being Lilin rather than "Adamite"             Quote Post by MuscleRobo » Wed May 31, 2023 12:52 pm Asunji_Yuko wrote: We know all EVAs are clones of Adam except for the main character's/Unit 01/Test Type/Shogohki/"The Devil," who is a clone of Lilith. What's the significance of this? I don't recall seeing it discussed in much detail or matter much in the original series Thoughts?

[]: Re: Significance of EVA-01 ("The Devil") being Lilin rather than "Adamite"             Quote Post by 2Lacissal2 » Wed May 31, 2023 1:40 pm I think some of the God/Devil language used for Eva 01 is probably a bit of inspiration from Mazinger Z. In the first volume of the Mazinger manga (I’m 90% sure this volume in particular was referenced by one of the Eva staff) Mazinger is described as ‘will it be an Angel that saves humanity? Or a devil that destroys the world?’ in language very similar to what Fuyutsuki says about Eva 01 during EoE.

[]: Re: Significance of EVA-01 ("The Devil") being Lilin rather than "Adamite"             Quote Post by Blockio » Wed May 31, 2023 3:51 pm Yes, Mazinger also does that, but I don't think the notion holds that it is a reference to a reference in another work, so much as simply being a reference to biblical concepts; not like there is any shortage of those in NGE otherwise
